mappings.rationale: Directly addresses the philosophy of art, diverse conceptions of art, and theories concerning the nature and functions of art — the core topics of this section.
Covers formalist, expressive, and pragmatic theories of art and the philosophy of aesthetic experience, central to section 611's treatment of diverse art theories.
Examines philosophical theories about beauty in art and nature, directly relevant to the philosophy of art and criteria for classifying fine art.
Addresses the classification and periodization of arts, style analysis, and the distinction between art historical categories — relevant to classification of arts and style.
Explores the distinction between useful art and fine art, and the classification of art by function versus aesthetics — key topics in the section's classification framework.
Directly relevant to the section's treatment of the creative process in the making of works of art.
Addresses period style in the arts across literature, music, and visual arts — relevant to the section's treatment of style varieties and the characterization of arts by cultural circumstances.
Examines postmodernist aesthetic theories and style across multiple art forms, relevant to the section's discussion of period styles and diverse theories of art.
Explores avant-garde movements that challenged conventional classifications of art and blurred boundaries between art forms, relevant to the section's treatment of style and art classification.
Covers Romanticism as a major period style across literature, music, and visual arts — directly relevant to the section's discussion of period and school styles.
Relevant to the characterization of primitive, folk, and popular arts by reference to cultural and social circumstances — a key topic in section 611.
Explores the role of imagination in artistic creation and aesthetic experience, directly relevant to the section's treatment of the creative process.
Examines art movements that fundamentally challenged prevailing theories of art's nature and function, relevant to the section's discussion of diverse conceptions of art.
Relevant to the section's discussion of primitive, folk, and popular arts as classified by the cultural character of their audience.
Addresses mimetic, expressive, formalist, and pragmatic theories of literature as an art — directly relevant to the section's treatment of diverse art theories.
Explores Frankfurt School approaches to art theory including the relationship between art, society, and culture — relevant to theories of art's functions and social context.
Addresses current debates about what counts as art and how contemporary works challenge traditional classifications — relevant to the section's treatment of scope and classification of art.
Explores how modern art movements redefined conceptions of art and created new categories, relevant to classification and style discussions in this section.
